title: Discover and invoke the INGEST agent contract
preconditions: A compatible Tally executable is on PATH and the invoking process runs under the owner's trusted OS identity.
postconditions: The delegated workflow uses only public contracts and can distinguish success, block, unsupported input, compatibility failure, interruption, and safe retry without parsing prose.
main_flow: 1. The client requests INGEST help and versioned machine-readable schemas. 2. INGEST reports supported operations, Format Variants, safe limits, result shapes, stable errors, and exit codes without reading a source or mutating LEDGER. 3. The client constructs non-interactive structured input for preview, inspect, approve, commit, resume, status, abandon, or cleanup. 4. INGEST validates compatibility and input before processing. 5. Exactly one structured business result is written to stdout; metadata-only diagnostics use stderr; the documented exit code reflects the result. 6. Lost mutation output is recovered through the original batch and idempotency identities.
alt_flows: 1a. Operation or schema is unknown -> return discoverable help and stable usage failure
2a. Skill, adapter, INGEST, or LEDGER contract versions are incompatible -> fail before source processing or mutation
3a. Input is malformed or contains forbidden unknown fields -> reject before processing
5a. Expected domain failure occurs -> return one module-scoped structured error and stable non-zero exit
6a. Client constructs a new identity after losing output -> refuse unsafe continuation and direct it to status/resume
metadata: {"depth_tier":2,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The Personal Finance Owner delegates statement ingestion to an AI Agent Host or invokes Tally directly.","status":"draft"}
